segments.text: For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He agreed to pay them a denarius[^121] for the day and sent them into his vineyard.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: About the third hour[^122] he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘You also go into my vineyard,’ he said, ‘and I will pay you whatever is right.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
So they went.
He went out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour[^123] and did the same thing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: About the eleventh hour[^124] he went out and found still others standing around. ‘Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?’ he asked.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
‘Because no one has hired us,’ they answered.
So he told them, ‘You also go into my vineyard.’[^125]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, ‘Call the workers and pay them their wages, starting with the last ones hired and moving on to the first.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The workers who were hired about the eleventh hour came and each received a denarius.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So when the original workers came, they assumed they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarius.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: On receiving their pay, they began to grumble against the landowner.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘These men who were hired last worked only one hour,’ they said, ‘and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden and the scorching heat of the day.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But he answered one of them, ‘Friend, I am not being unfair to you. Did you not agree with me on one denarius?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Take your pay and go. I want to give this last man the same as I gave you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Do I not have the right to do as I please with what is mine? Or are you envious because I am generous?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So the last will be first, and the first will be last.
segments.speaker: Jesus

text: The Third Prediction of the Passion

segments.text: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, He took the twelve disciples aside and said,
Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and scribes. They will condemn Him to death
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and will deliver Him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified. And on the third day He will be raised to life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the mother of Zebedee’s sons came to Jesus with her sons and knelt down to make a request of Him.
What do you want?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
He inquired.
She answered,
Declare that in Your kingdom one of these two sons of mine may sit at Your right hand, and the other at Your left.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You do not know what you are asking,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
Can you drink the cup I am going to drink?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: We can,
segments.speaker: The brothers

segments.text: the brothers answered.
You will indeed drink My cup,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: [^128] Jesus said.
But to sit at My right or left is not Mine to grant. These seats belong to those for whom My Father has prepared them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the ten heard about this, they were indignant with the two brothers.
But Jesus called them aside and said,
You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their superiors exercise authority over them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It shall not be this way among you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave—
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life as a ransom for many.
segments.speaker: Jesus

text: The Blind Men by the Road

segments.text: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed Him.
And there were two blind men sitting beside the road. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out,
Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The crowd admonished them to be silent, but they cried out all the louder,
Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!
segments.speaker: The crowd

segments.text: Jesus stopped and called them.
What do you want Me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He asked.
Lord,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered,
let our eyes be opened.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes, and at once they received their sight and followed Him.
